title: Generalized Seizure (Convulsions)
symptoms: Uncontrollable jerking of arms and legs
immediate_steps: Note the time the seizure starts
Clear the area of any hard or sharp objects
Ease the person to the floor and place something soft under their head
Loosen tight clothing around the neck
Gently roll them onto their side to keep the airway clear and allow fluids to drain
Stay with them until the seizure ends and they are fully aware
Call emergency services if the seizure lasts >5 minutes, they have a second seizure, they are injured, pregnant, or it's their first seizure
avoid: Do not hold the person down or try to stop their movements
Do not put absolutely anything in their mouth (they cannot swallow their tongue)
Do not give food or water until they are fully alert
Do not attempt artificial respiration during the seizure

title: Focal (Partial) Seizure
symptoms: Repetitive movements (lip smacking, chewing, hand rubbing)
Altered sense of taste or smell
Confusion, but remaining on their feet
immediate_steps: Gently guide them away from hazards (stairs, traffic)
Stay with them until they return to complete awareness
Note the duration of the event
avoid: Do not grab them or yell (they may perceive it as a threat and become aggressive)
Do not try to snap them out of it
Do not restrain them unless they are in immediate danger
